messages.content: That script was incredibly slow! Please use your expertise to make the script execute faster! Also, please use the standard script indenting and straight quotes in our output.

I got this error:

6:35:55 PM	Error	
Exception: Range not found
clearFormatting	@ Code.gs:199

I also had to replace your quote marks and I may have done it wrongly. Can you please use the correct quote marks in your script?

The error is 6:41:10 PM	Error	
Exception: Range not found
clearFormatting	@ Code.gs:199

I got this error:

6:59:11 PM	Error	
Exception: Range not found
clearFormatting	@ Code.gs:207

I apologize for the confusion. The issue might be not having enough columns in the sheet or that the sheet contains merged cells. Based on the error message details, it appears that the error is occurring in the previous script I provided. Please make sure you are using the latest script in the previous response, the one with `resetFormatting()` function, as it has been adapted from the script you've provided and should work in the same working context.

If you still receive an error with the `resetFormatting()` function, please double-check your spreadsheet and ensure that there are no merged cells, as this could cause issues with the range operations. Additionally, ensure that the spreadsheet contains the required columns (C, H, M, and R).

If the issue persists and you believe it is not due to merged cells or missing columns, please provide more information about your spreadsheet, such as the number of rows, columns, and the format of the entries.

actually there are some merged rows that include the columns in my range. However, in the leftmost cell of those rows (column A) the value ends with "day" so I think those rows can be excluded by the script. Can you make the necessary adjustment?

The range error refers to this line:     sheet.getRangeList(formatRanges).clearFormat();
Somehow the model script did not have a similar error.  Can you format the script so the clearFormat() line is in the conditional?
That script works but it takes long to execute. I want you to simplify it to only work on columns C, H, M, and R, and to ignore the other columns.

That was great! Now the script needs to be expanded. After clearing the format of the cells, it should set the background of the same cells to the color "#DFE3E7" and the font to Calibri. Please keep in mind that execution speed is important and should be maximized.

Beautiful! Now there needs to be one further function in the script. The script needs to delete the content in all the cells in the D, I, N, and S columns if the cell to the left (that is, in columns C, H, M, and R and in the same row) does not have more than one character.

OK, we did it! The script works great, and now it's done. Now I need your help in allowing this script to be launched from a menu option inside google sheets. I've never done this before. Can you give me step-by-step instructions for what actions I need to take so that there is a menu item that allows me to run a function called "resetFormat"?
What would it look like if I added a second function fastLinkMaker, and a third function resetURLs  ?
should those quotes be double quotes or single quotes?

New idea: I need a script to first remove all all the values of all the cells in columns D, I, N, and S unless that text says “Status.” After all the deletion is done, I want the script to check if one cell to the right (so in columns C,H,M, and R) the cell has a RichText link. If it does, I want it to extract and copy the URL of that link one cell to the right, which would place it in the columns D, I, N, and S, which had been deleted. Please propose an efficient script that would do this.
Please create a maximally efficient and fast google sheets script that clears all the values in the columns D, I, N, and S , except when the value is "Status". Please call it "clearURL"
